tasks.description: Build the authoritative migration inventory before editing code: grep the full panes surface (`PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, `usePanes()`), enumerate all panes-related tests/mocks, capture the repo-wide `createContext` declaration inventory with the generic-aware regex, and capture the repo-wide exported hook inventory (`export const/function use...`) so the later audit includes recomposed context-access hook families outside the original context/provider directories.
tasks.executor_notes: No new inventory scope changes were needed. The rework reused the existing repo-wide context and hook inventory, then revalidated the final declaration list against the updated audit document.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. The inventory artifacts and grep commands support a broad initial repo scan for panes callers, context declarations, and exported hooks.

tasks.description: Implement `src/shared/state/panesStore.ts` as the new selector-first panes state surface, mirroring current pane behavior and provider-owned logic: preserve pane open/lock/dimension/effective-height/`activeTaskId` semantics, fold in the current pane lock policy bootstrap behavior, add an explicit singleton lifecycle contract including an idempotent bootstrap path and a test reset helper, and define the intended behavior for outside-bootstrap reads so tests can assert it intentionally.
tasks.executor_notes: The panes store lifecycle contract and selector-first API were unchanged by this pass. No regression was introduced while fixing the audit and guard follow-up items.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. `panesStore.ts` implements the promised selector-first store, bootstrap path, and reset helpers.

tasks.description: Replace `PanesProvider` in the app provider tree with the panes-store bootstrap wiring while preserving mount order and initialization timing relative to `ProjectProvider`, `RealtimeProvider`, `IncomingTasksProvider`, and `AgentChatProvider`; then migrate every former panes consumer, including high-level panes readers, from `usePanes()` to dedicated selectors or explicitly narrow grouped selectors without recreating a broad compatibility facade.
tasks.executor_notes: No panes consumer or provider-tree changes were needed. Production panes access remains selector-based and the bootstrap slot remains in the established provider position.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. `AppProviders.tsx` preserves the panes bootstrap slot and production consumers now read from the store instead of `usePanes()`.

tasks.description: Migrate panes-related tests and mocks to the store/bootstrap model, replace the old `PanesContext` test coverage with store/bootstrap-focused coverage, ensure singleton reset/lifecycle behavior is exercised, and then delete the old panes context surface entirely so no production file imports `PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, or `usePanes()` afterward.
tasks.executor_notes: The legacy panes API stayed removed from production usage throughout this pass. Verification later in T8 reconfirmed there are no production `PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, or `usePanes()` imports.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. The legacy panes context file is deleted and production grep confirms the old surface is gone.

tasks.description: Run the exhaustive context audit using the repo-wide declaration and exported-hook inventories: compute field counts using imported local type expansion, compute external production consumer counts while excluding same-file helper wiring/tests/mocks, classify every provider declaration and recomposed context-access hook (`migrate`, `narrow`, `keep`, or `out-of-scope`), and commit the results to `docs/state_management_context_audit.md` with rationale and any explicit exemptions.
tasks.executor_notes: Updated the audit document to reflect the current declaration-by-declaration inventory instead of the older umbrella summaries. The document now records 53 total declarations, including the split Lightbox/ImageGenerationForm contexts and four test-only declarations (`TabsContext`, `SegmentContext`, and the two existing `SelectContext` mocks), so the appendix and grouped tables reconcile to the same current snapshot.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Needs follow-up. The audit document exists but is not a complete final per-declaration inventory of the current `createContext` set.

tasks.description: For every non-pane surface that exceeds both audit thresholds under the documented counting rules, migrate or narrow it in this same branch using the smallest viable fix: replace recomposed compatibility hooks with narrower exports where possible, move truly wide mutable state to selector-based access where necessary, and leave below-threshold or local UI token contexts documented rather than redesigned.
tasks.executor_notes: No additional non-pane migrations were required on this pass. The previously narrowed over-threshold non-pane surfaces remain the audited end state.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. The over-threshold non-pane surfaces identified by the audit were narrowed or split in code, and production broad-hook callers were removed.

tasks.description: Add the permanent automated fat-context declaration guard using the same field-count rule documented in the audit: implement the repo-level check (preferably under `scripts/quality/`), keep its scope limited to new over-threshold `createContext(...)` declarations, wire it into an automated repo command or CI quality path, and document the mechanism plus any allowlist/exemption handling in the audit doc and/or repo docs.
tasks.executor_notes: Hardened the fat-context guard so identifier callees are resolved through TypeScript import symbols rather than literal identifier text. That keeps named-import aliases and `React.createContext(...)` namespace/default-import forms covered under the same counting rule documented in the audit.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Needs follow-up. The guard is wired into repo scripts, but its matcher misses `React.createContext(...)` declarations.

tasks.description: Run end-to-end verification and close the branch: write a short throwaway reproduction script that exercises the original broad panes subscription/problem shape and/or the targeted state transitions, run it to confirm the new store/selectors behave as intended, delete the script afterward, run the most relevant focused tests first, then run `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm test`, grep-verify that no production file imports `PanesContext`, `PanesProvider`, or `usePanes()`, and if any test or typecheck fails, fix the code and re-run until clean. For deferred-human runtime-only checks such as telemetry-overlay rerender confirmation and small-viewport exclusivity behavior, mark them `skipped` on first encounter with executor notes citing the sandbox/runtime/browser limitation.
tasks.executor_notes: Ran and deleted a short repro script to confirm the two review failures were addressed, then reran the required verification suite. `npm run check:fat-contexts`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, the production grep for removed panes APIs, and the full `npm test` suite all passed after the fixes.
tasks.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Verification evidence covers the reproduction script, clean typecheck, clean test suite, and production grep cleanup; deferred-human runtime checks were handled appropriately.

watch_items: Panes are already split across `PaneLockPolicyContext`, `PaneLayoutContext`, and `PaneSelectionContext`; the real hotspot is the recomposed `usePanes()` surface plus provider-owned bootstrap state. Judge success by deletion of that broad facade, not by the stale 'single fat context' wording.
Do not recreate a broad panes compatibility API on top of Zustand. High-level panes consumers must move to dedicated selectors or tightly scoped grouped selectors only where genuinely necessary.

Preserve mount order and initialization timing in `src/app/providers/AppProviders.tsx`; panes bootstrap is load-bearing relative to `ProjectProvider`, `RealtimeProvider`, `IncomingTasksProvider`, and `AgentChatProvider`.
Preserve pane-lock policy behavior, including mobile/tablet exclusivity, persisted `paneLocks` hydration, and tasks-pane open/lock coupling from the current bootstrap logic.
Define the panes-store singleton lifecycle contract explicitly. Add an idempotent bootstrap and a reset helper to avoid cross-test leakage; do not leave behavior to incidental module state.
Audit for side effects currently embedded in panes/provider logic before deletion, including any open/lock transitions that may trigger analytics or downstream coordination.

The context audit must start from a repo-wide exported hook inventory, not path-limited guesses. Include surfaces such as `useProject`, media-lightbox safe hooks, travel-between-images hooks, and `useImageGenerationFormContexts`.

Use the documented field-count rule consistently in both the audit doc and the permanent guard: expand imported local repository types recursively until a documented cutoff; third-party or opaque types count as one.
Consumer counts for wrapper hooks must use external production consumers only. Exclude same-file helper composition, tests, and mocks from threshold decisions.
Keep scope tight during the audit: framework/local UI token contexts may be marked `out-of-scope` or `keep` with rationale rather than migrated.
Every audited non-pane surface above both thresholds must be migrated or narrowed in this branch; do not stop after panes if additional over-threshold surfaces are found.
Do not worsen unrelated debt-watch items elsewhere in the repo; ignore unrelated dirty-worktree changes unless they directly conflict with this milestone.

Deferred-human checks are first-class `skipped` outcomes in this sandbox. If browser/runtime/device/manual verification is required, skip immediately with concrete blocker evidence rather than blocking or retrying.

sense_checks.executor_note: No panes consumer migration changes were needed on this pass, and provider-tree mount order was left untouched.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed. Production panes consumers now use store selectors and `AppProviders.tsx` mounts the bootstrap boundary in the prior slot.

sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed. The legacy panes API is removed from production code.

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed by updating the document to a declaration-by-declaration 53-entry inventory with matching grouped tables and descriptor snapshot entries, including the split Lightbox/ImageGenerationForm declarations and the additional test-only mock contexts.

sense_checks.executor_note: No over-threshold non-pane migration regressed on this pass; the previously narrowed/split surfaces remain the audited state.

sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed by running a throwaway repro script and deleting it afterward, then passing `npm run check:fat-contexts`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, the production grep for removed panes APIs, and the full `npm test` suite.
sense_checks.verdict: Confirmed. Verification evidence covers the scripted repro, clean typecheck/test runs, and production grep cleanup; runtime-only checks remain deferred human work.

meta_commentary: Execution should follow the concrete hot path, not the stale prose framing. The repository already split panes into three contexts; the performance win comes from deleting `usePanes()` and provider-owned pane state in favor of narrow store selectors and explicit bootstrap state. Treat T1 as the authoritative checklist generator so later work is inventory-backed rather than sample-driven. During the audit, keep the counting contract disciplined: imported local types must be expanded for field counts, while consumer counts must exclude same-file composition/tests/mocks. The permanent guard is intentionally narrower than the audit: it blocks new fat `createContext(...)` declarations only, while recomposed wrapper-hook anti-patterns are handled as milestone migration work, not permanent lint scope. The final verification task must include a disposable reproduction script, focused test passes before full suite runs, and immediate `skipped` handling for browser/runtime/device-only checks with explicit blocker notes.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Build complete repo-wide inventory for panes, contexts, and wrapper hooks
Introduce selector-first panes store with lifecycle contract
Migrate all panes callers to narrow selectors and delete old panes surface
Audit provider declarations and recomposed access hooks with accurate counting rules
Migrate or narrow every over-threshold non-pane surface
Prove the milestone end-to-end with tests, grep checks, and required artifacts

validation.completeness_notes: All seven approved plan steps are covered. The split between T3 and T4 is deliberate: Step 3 in the approved plan spans both production consumer migration and test/mock deletion work, so it maps to two execution tasks for clarity. Deferred-human runtime checks remain intentionally handled inside T8 as `skipped` if encountered because the sandbox cannot produce browser/device evidence.

baseline_test_note: No supported test runner detected on PATH (looked for: pytest). Configure test_command in state config to specify one.
